# Iteration 005: SEARCH MECHANISMS

## Focus
SEARCH MECHANISMS: FTS, vector, hybrid, ranking algorithms. Query processing, shortcuts, relevance scoring.

## Findings
### Finding 1: Mex does not implement an actual FTS, vector, or hybrid retrieval engine
- **Source**: [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L24), [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L72), [external/src/cli.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/cli.ts#L28), [external/package.json](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/package.json#L43)
- **What it does**: Mex positions itself as a markdown scaffold plus CLI for drift detection, pre-analysis, and sync. Its command surface is `setup`, `check`, `init`, `sync`, `pattern`, `watch`, and `commands`; its runtime dependencies are markdown/git/glob tooling only. I did not find any search index, database, embeddings, sqlite FTS, vector store, or fusion module in the inspected source tree.
- **Why it matters for us**: For a search-mechanisms comparison, the key result is absence: Mex is not a retrieval backend competing with Spec Kit Memory. It is a scaffold-validation and repair system.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: Mex query processing is claim extraction from markdown, not user-query retrieval
- **Source**: [external/src/drift/claims.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/claims.ts#L7), [external/src/drift/claims.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/claims.ts#L36), [external/src/drift/checkers/path.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/path.ts#L8), [external/src/drift/checkers/command.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/command.ts#L5)
- **What it does**: Mex parses markdown AST nodes and extracts four claim kinds only: `path`, `command`, `dependency`, and `version`. It uses regex guards for routes/placeholders/code snippets, tracks heading context and negation, and then runs deterministic validators over those extracted claims.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is useful static documentation analysis, but it is not semantic retrieval, lexical ranking, or hybrid search. The “query” in Mex is effectively a checker over authored markdown claims, not a free-form user information need.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 3: Mex’s only search shortcuts are static routing and fuzzy filesystem rescue
- **Source**: [external/ROUTER.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/ROUTER.md#L3), [external/ROUTER.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/ROUTER.md#L43), [external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L67), [external/src/drift/checkers/path.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/path.ts#L41)
- **What it does**: Mex shortcuts work in two deterministic ways. First, `ROUTER.md` hard-routes task types to specific context files and pattern index entries. Second, when sync finds a missing path, `brief-builder.ts` lists nearby directory contents and does a glob-based same-extension scan; `path.ts` also recursively glob-searches bare filenames up to depth 5.
- **Why it matters for us**: Mex is optimized for “show me the likely file neighborhood” rather than “retrieve the most relevant memories/documents.” That makes it cheap and explainable for repair, but weak for general search relevance.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 4: Mex scoring ranks scaffold health, not retrieval relevance
- **Source**: [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L74), [external/src/drift/scoring.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/scoring.ts#L3), [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L47), [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L155)
- **What it does**: Mex computes a single drift score from issue severities: `100 - 10*errors - 3*warnings - 1*infos`. `sync` uses that score as the optimization target across fix/recheck cycles and reports score deltas after each loop.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is a repo-health metric, not a search-ranking model. It can tell an agent whether docs are getting cleaner, but it cannot order retrieved memories by relevance or confidence.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 5: Spec Kit Memory already has the real search stack Mex lacks
- **Source**: [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L482), [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L542), [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L771), [pipeline/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md#L34), [query-router.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/query-router.ts#L23), [sqlite-fts.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/sqlite-fts.ts#L147), [bm25-index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/bm25-index.ts#L45), [vector-index-queries.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-queries.ts#L168), [hybrid-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/hybrid-search.ts#L611), [hybrid-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/hybrid-search.ts#L1006), [stage2-fusion.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/stage2-fusion.ts#L10)
- **What it does**: Our runtime validates `query` vs `concepts` vs `cursor`, executes a four-stage retrieval pipeline, routes simple/moderate/complex queries into channel subsets, runs sqlite-vec cosine search with decay-aware ordering, runs weighted FTS5 BM25 with per-column weights, maintains an in-memory BM25 lane with stemming/stop-word filtering, fuses multi-channel results with adaptive weighting and RRF, and then applies Stage 2 signal scoring plus Stage 3 reranking/MMR.
- **Why it matters for us**: Mex is not a search upgrade for Spec Kit Memory. The reusable value is a static drift-validation layer for markdown claims and doc/code sync, not a replacement for retrieval or ranking.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

## Sources Consulted

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.86
- Questions addressed: whether Mex has any real FTS/vector/hybrid engine, how Mex processes “queries,” what shortcuts it uses instead of retrieval, how Mex scoring works, and how that compares to Spec Kit Memory’s current search stack
- Questions answered: Mex has no source-level retrieval engine beyond static markdown claim parsing and filesystem globbing; its “ranking” is only drift-health scoring; our system already provides true vector, lexical, hybrid, routing, and reranking behavior; the reusable Mex slice is a static claim-validation layer, not search replacement

## Reflection
- What worked: reading `package.json`, `cli.ts`, `claims.ts`, `brief-builder.ts`, and the search pipeline files side by side made the boundary very clear between Mex’s scaffold-validation model and our retrieval stack
- What did not work: searching for hidden Mex search internals beyond drift/sync/scaffold routes; the implementation consistently bottoms out in AST parsing, globbing, and score-by-severity rather than any indexed retrieval path

## Recommended Next Focus
Investigate whether the best reusable search-side addition is a new static “documentation claim lane” for Spec Kit Memory: extract path/command/dependency/version claims from markdown and score drift against the filesystem and manifests, but keep `memory_search` as the actual retrieval backend.

# Iteration 005: SEARCH MECHANISMS

## Focus
SEARCH MECHANISMS: FTS, vector, hybrid, ranking algorithms. Query processing, shortcuts, relevance scoring.

## Findings
### Finding 1: Mex does not implement an actual FTS, vector, or hybrid retrieval engine
- **Source**: [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L24), [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L72), [external/src/cli.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/cli.ts#L28), [external/package.json](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/package.json#L43)
- **What it does**: Mex positions itself as a markdown scaffold plus CLI for drift detection, pre-analysis, and sync. Its command surface is `setup`, `check`, `init`, `sync`, `pattern`, `watch`, and `commands`; its runtime dependencies are markdown/git/glob tooling only. I did not find any search index, database, embeddings, sqlite FTS, vector store, or fusion module in the inspected source tree.
- **Why it matters for us**: For a search-mechanisms comparison, the key result is absence: Mex is not a retrieval backend competing with Spec Kit Memory. It is a scaffold-validation and repair system.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: Mex query processing is claim extraction from markdown, not user-query retrieval
- **Source**: [external/src/drift/claims.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/claims.ts#L7), [external/src/drift/claims.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/claims.ts#L36), [external/src/drift/checkers/path.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/path.ts#L8), [external/src/drift/checkers/command.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/command.ts#L5)
- **What it does**: Mex parses markdown AST nodes and extracts four claim kinds only: `path`, `command`, `dependency`, and `version`. It uses regex guards for routes/placeholders/code snippets, tracks heading context and negation, and then runs deterministic validators over those extracted claims.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is useful static documentation analysis, but it is not semantic retrieval, lexical ranking, or hybrid search. The “query” in Mex is effectively a checker over authored markdown claims, not a free-form user information need.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 3: Mex’s only search shortcuts are static routing and fuzzy filesystem rescue
- **Source**: [external/ROUTER.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/ROUTER.md#L3), [external/ROUTER.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/ROUTER.md#L43), [external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L67), [external/src/drift/checkers/path.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/path.ts#L41)
- **What it does**: Mex shortcuts work in two deterministic ways. First, `ROUTER.md` hard-routes task types to specific context files and pattern index entries. Second, when sync finds a missing path, `brief-builder.ts` lists nearby directory contents and does a glob-based same-extension scan; `path.ts` also recursively glob-searches bare filenames up to depth 5.
- **Why it matters for us**: Mex is optimized for “show me the likely file neighborhood” rather than “retrieve the most relevant memories/documents.” That makes it cheap and explainable for repair, but weak for general search relevance.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 4: Mex scoring ranks scaffold health, not retrieval relevance
- **Source**: [external/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L74), [external/src/drift/scoring.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/scoring.ts#L3), [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L47), [external/src/sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L155)
- **What it does**: Mex computes a single drift score from issue severities: `100 - 10*errors - 3*warnings - 1*infos`. `sync` uses that score as the optimization target across fix/recheck cycles and reports score deltas after each loop.
- **Why it matters for us**: This is a repo-health metric, not a search-ranking model. It can tell an agent whether docs are getting cleaner, but it cannot order retrieved memories by relevance or confidence.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 5: Spec Kit Memory already has the real search stack Mex lacks
- **Source**: [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L482), [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L542), [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts#L771), [pipeline/README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md#L34), [query-router.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/query-router.ts#L23), [sqlite-fts.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/sqlite-fts.ts#L147), [bm25-index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/bm25-index.ts#L45), [vector-index-queries.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-queries.ts#L168), [hybrid-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/hybrid-search.ts#L611), [hybrid-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/hybrid-search.ts#L1006), [stage2-fusion.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/stage2-fusion.ts#L10)
- **What it does**: Our runtime validates `query` vs `concepts` vs `cursor`, executes a four-stage retrieval pipeline, routes simple/moderate/complex queries into channel subsets, runs sqlite-vec cosine search with decay-aware ordering, runs weighted FTS5 BM25 with per-column weights, maintains an in-memory BM25 lane with stemming/stop-word filtering, fuses multi-channel results with adaptive weighting and RRF, and then applies Stage 2 signal scoring plus Stage 3 reranking/MMR.
- **Why it matters for us**: Mex is not a search upgrade for Spec Kit Memory. The reusable value is a static drift-validation layer for markdown claims and doc/code sync, not a replacement for retrieval or ranking.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high
